verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II membrane protein resident in the Golgi apparatus. Its N-terminal hydrophobic signal sequence directs it to the Golgi and remains uncleaved, functioning as a transmembrane anchor in the Golgi lumen-facing orientation (ER-Golgi resident, not plasma membrane). As a glycosyltransferase, its active site faces the Golgi lumen. No documented cancer ecto-pool, cell-death surface display, developmental surface restriction, activation-induced translocation, lysosomal-exocytosis cycling, stable PM-partner tethering, or minority PM pool. Not a substrate for surface-directed therapeutics.
